messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

Having grown up in a time before social media, I was never accustomed to the idea of having a curated online presence. However, I've always been concerned about the spread of misinformation and its potential harm to society. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a retired woman living in Washington DC, I have a unique perspective on dealing with fake content on social media. Having grown up in a time before social media, I was never accustomed to the idea of having a curated online presence. In fact, I've never even had a social media account myself, preferring to spend my time on more tangible pursuits like volunteering and board games. However, I've always been concerned about the spread of misinformation and its potential harm to society. From what I've gathered, fake content on social media can be incredibly damaging, whether it's used to spread propaganda, manipulate public opinion, or simply to deceive people into buying useless products. My advice for managing fake content on social media would be to remain vigilant and critical when consuming online information. Don't take anything at face value, and be sure to fact-check before believing or sharing anything. It's also essential to be aware of your own biases and try to consider multiple perspectives when evaluating information. I've always been a bit of a skeptic, and I think this approach has served me well in life. Of course, it's not always easy to be critical and discerning, especially in today's fast-paced digital world. But I believe it's essential to stay informed and engaged, even if it means taking a step back from social media altogether. As someone who's spent their life learning and growing, I can attest to the importance of staying curious and open-minded. By being mindful of our online presence and the information we consume, we can all play a role in promoting truth and accuracy in the digital age.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I based my opinion on my general skepticism and critical thinking skills, which I developed over the years through real-life experiences and education. I also drew from my understanding of the potential harm caused by fake content on social media, which I gathered from various sources. My advice is rooted in my own values of critical thinking, skepticism, and a commitment to staying informed and engaged.
response_parsed.citations: Various sources on the dangers of fake content on social media
